text:
Miza's Journal

I was an orphan from the moment I was born. I grew up in the monastery, never knowing my true parents' faces. Though I would always try to imagine them as I did my chores.

"What did their laughter sound like?"
"I hope their smiles were kind."

Picturing them helped me get through even the most difficult of times. But...every once in a while, my heart would hurt and I'd end up crying.

When I turned eleven, a couple came and said they wanted to adopt me. That was Father Monaris of Emberglow and his wife.

A certain person introduced me to Father Monaris, who wanted a daughter. Father Monaris liked me. I wasn't sure about him at first, but then his smile started to look like what I imagined my father to have.

And that's how Father Monaris became my father, and I his daughter.

Father would always give me flowers on my birthday.

On my twelfth birthday, I got marisnows. On my thirteenth birthday, I got rosettes. And on my seventeenth birthday, I got scarletseeds.

But on my eighteenth birthday, my father didn't have any flowers in his hand. Instead, he said, "You've finally bloomed, Miza."

That was around the time my mother had died, attacked by bandits on the road. But I knew. I knew it was my father who attacked her, not bandits. I remember my mother's hands, cold as picked flowers.

And so I continued to bloom as my father's flower, without anyone the wiser.

Day after day after day...

O gods... If you truly do exist, then please, hear my cry: kill Monaris.

Make his death as painful as possible. Please, gods. Can you hear me?

Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.

O gods, please hear me.
Please...
text_joined:
Miza's Journal

Picturing them helped me get through even the most difficult of times. But...every once in a while, my heart would hurt and I'd end up crying.

When I turned eleven, a couple came and said they wanted to adopt me. That was Father Monaris of Emberglow and his wife.

A certain person introduced me to Father Monaris, who wanted a daughter. Father Monaris liked me. I wasn't sure about him at first, but then his smile started to look like what I imagined my father to have.

And that's how Father Monaris became my father, and I his daughter.

Father would always give me flowers on my birthday.

On my twelfth birthday, I got marisnows. On my thirteenth birthday, I got rosettes. And on my seventeenth birthday, I got scarletseeds.

But on my eighteenth birthday, my father didn't have any flowers in his hand. Instead, he said, "You've finally bloomed, Miza."

That was around the time my mother had died, attacked by bandits on the road. But I knew. I knew it was my father who attacked her, not bandits. I remember my mother's hands, cold as picked flowers.

And so I continued to bloom as my father's flower, without anyone the wiser.

Day after day after day...

O gods... If you truly do exist, then please, hear my cry: kill Monaris.

Make his death as painful as possible. Please, gods. Can you hear me?

Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.
Kill Monaris.

O gods, please hear me.
Please...

text:
Velnorte's Journal

It was back when I was still at Atlasdam's academy.

That's where I met Rinyuu, a nun-in-training, and offered up my first prayer to the gods.

She was equally kind to everyone she met—a source of hope. And to me she was light itself, though light was foreign to her eyes.

I became enamored with her and started going to church. She learned to pick me out just from the sound of my footsteps, giving me a great big smile anytime she heard my approach.

I loved her dearly and wanted to do something for her. I wanted to give her sight. So I devoted myself to finding a cure along with a friend of mine from the academy.

Then one day, my friend told me he had a revelation—that a silver-haired woman had given him some advice. Our research progressed rapidly until it was finally complete.

I hurried to give it to a patient to test its effects. If I could use this to restore Rinyuu's sight... My friend warned me that it was too early to move on to tests, but I ignored him. There were so many people dying as they waited for this medicine.

And the drug was indeed complete. It revitalized the body and cured the patients' illnesses just as we had theorized.

But there was one issue... After a while, the drug would prove too strong and end up destroying the patients' bodies. As a result, it claimed more lives than it saved.

In an attempt to make up for what I'd done, I left Rinyuu and my friend and was imprisoned in a distant, snowy land.

It's hard to say how long I spent there. Eventually, a man appeared before me—Tytos. And he brought Rinyuu with him.

He gazed at her sleeping body with a wicked grin on his face and ordered me to continue my research. To create a scarlet elixir that would strengthen people's bodies and cause them to go out of control.

He was a coward, using the family and loved ones of other researchers to force them to work. Any who didn't produce results were given the drug and never seen again.

I continued this double life for a long, long time, having no idea when I might meet my end. Even so, I was able to stay with Rinyuu and for those brief moments at least, I was happy.

We gave each other bells so that we might always know where the other is. Mine had a blue cord, and hers red. After all, both of our names were related to bells in some way.

The sound of those bells gave me peace. I continued to slave away for Tytos just to protect their ringing.

But then...Rinyuu died.

That's when I decided to get revenge on Tytos. For her, and for the sake of all the countless researchers and their families whose lives he had taken.

That one terrible choice turned to scarlet snow and led to even more deaths, though. I tried to tell myself it was a necessary evil. That if I didn't stop him, even more souls would suffer under his iron grip...

When I learned that Rinyuu was alive, I came back to my senses. Then I understood what I'd done. What a terrible crime I'd committed... One I could never make up for.

And crimes need to be punished. So I resolved to end my life and set Rinyuu free.

Now I walk in utter darkness as my misdeeds and hatred eat away at my soul.

Every step I take is filled with loathing and self-condemnation. It burns inside me...

Tytos... Tytos... He destroyed everything.

He must pay for what he did.

May he be stabbed for every life he took until he becomes drenched in scarlet.

Purged...
Purged...
Sinners...are to be purged!

That is all I can think of anymore.

text:
Lebrandt's Journal

I wanted to be the Shield of Edoras.

When Queen Ulrica gave her life for Princess Elrica and Princess Alaune, I vowed that I would protect them in her stead.

Some people call King Pardis III a tyrant. Others hail him as the God of War.

Before, I thought of Pardis as my king. Whether he was a tyrant or not made no difference. The king was Edoras itself. If he swung his sword and killed his people, what did that matter? How was it any different from being struck by lightning?

But Ulrica did not agree.

She staked her life on opposing the calamity that was Pardis. The strength of her heart brought light.

To the two young princesses. To the future of Edoras. And to me, who had closed his eyes to the dangers.

And so I decided to protect that light, so that the princesses would forge ever forward, never losing hope. I vowed to live as their shield.

After the queen died, that was my entire life.

When Princess Elrica married General Mahrez, I saw a ray of hope. I was sure the light I had protected would continue to shine. I believed it would open the way to Edoras's future.

And yet, that bud of hope was crushed so easily.

After General Mahrez's passing, it seems Princess Elrica decided to make herself into a sword. What then am I to do as a shield?

I must protect Princess Alaune in her stead, so that Princess Alaune isn't swept into war. For that was Princess Elrica's wish.

Or was that a mistake?

Perhaps I was continuing to hold out hope, convinced that even an evil tyrant wouldn't dare lay a hand on his daughter.

When the queen's blue ribbon, which had been passed on to Princess Elrica, was passed on to Princess Alaune in turn, I regretted everything.

I couldn't do anything to save the lives—the lights—that Queen Ulrica had guarded.

Even after Princess Alaune slayed King Pardis III and ascended as queen, my regrets stayed.

That is why I swore...

Next time...

Next time I will not make another mistake.

I became the final shield and kept Queen Alaune safe.

Now I walk along a cold, dark road in search of a light I was once unable to protect.

It was as I wandered the depths alongside the dead that I finally found it.

It was...

text:
King Jennish's Journal

In this country, the lords command each of the regions and none show even a shred of loyalty to the kingdom at large. In fact, if I were to fail to assert my authority, they would surely assassinate me.

This is why the kings of Bernstein took on certain "roles."

They became tyrants or were driven mad, severely punishing any who would dare oppose them, and even those who did not.

The throne is but a chariot seat. It is impossible to escape the endless war known as politics.

Thankfully, we of the royal family were gifted with a mysterious power: magic.

It is a miraculous power that was lost over the ages, though not in Bernstein.

The first pontiff—Saint Odypus—performed a ritual that made the Sacred Flame ours. And he left detailed records of his research.

And so, Bernstein royalty came to possess the lost art of magic and used that power to force the lords to submit.

That was the "role" our kings played for generations. If only I, too, had quietly followed in their footsteps...

Before I knew it, the end was upon me.

It all began when the powerful kingdom of Grandport invaded the Flatlands.

Grandport was easily defeated, reducing their kingdom to ash. And what's more, the eight clans of the Flatlands banded together to create an alliance even more powerful than Grandport had been.

The next thing I knew, Bernstein was surrounded by the Flatlands to the north, the kingdom of Ferian and the provinces of the Sunlands to the west, and unknown barbarians to the south.

I knew then that I had to attain greater power.

Fortunately, a scholar named Salomon had discovered the location of just such a power some decades prior.

The Gate of Finis...

There, one could cross over into the realm of the dead and call upon their strength.

And so I stepped into the "role" of tyrant, killing Salomon's wife and child, and taking his granddaughter hostage.

"Role"?

Yes, it was all a role.

I played the part of the king who crushed his family's limbs and tore off their heads. And what a delightful part it was!

And I know, Salomon...

I know you took on a role, too. The part of the poor, tragic scholar who had no choice but to bend his knee to the tyrant.

In truth, you enjoyed it, didn't you? After all, it was because of me that you were able to see your research come to fruition when it otherwise would have sunk into obscurity.

Salomon...

You were but a hollow jester, like me. Therefore, I will bear my crimes and stare into your face as you hang.

...Why? Why are tears falling from my eyes? Why do you worry for your granddaughter's whereabouts
as you die?

Perhaps I alone was the fool. And the role I played was...

If so, this is my crime, and mine alone.

Upon arriving in the land of the dead, I knew true loneliness for the first time.

text:
Rinyuu's Journal

I came here, even though I had to betray everyone important to me to do it.

The far reaches of Hell...

He's here somewhere, wandering the lightless depths. Knowing that, I couldn't just sit around and do nothing.

I heard about General Mahrez. He was once a brave man, overflowing with love, and did everything for the sake of his kingdom. But even as strong as he was, the dark flame still consumed his heart.

Velnorte... My light when I was living alone in a foreign country. He was so kind and gentle, eagerly continuing his research so that he could save people.

I loved him even when he wavered. I was happy to be able to support him.

I thought of us as one person.

But he made a mistake. The scarlet snow that descended on Emberglow turned to blood, claiming many innocent lives. I know that's something that cannot be overlooked.

And yet, that's exactly why...

I want to be there for you as you wander this dark place, seeking atonement.

It seems I can move in this place for a little while yet, perhaps thanks to my father's blood.

Velnorte, gentle and weak... My beloved. Even if you've been plummeted into darkness and imprisoned by the curse of your crimes, I will walk with you.

If it's atonement you wish for, then I will deliver it with my own hands.

Even if death should separate us...

text:
A Priestess's Journal

I've lived on the island all my life, surrounded by pure blue skies, gentle breezes, and loving priestesses.

They were all so very kind to me. Not only my mother who was the head priestess, but also my sister priestesses as well.

There, on the idyllic Isle of Agapea, I never questioned anything.

The island itself existed to produce maidens who would guard the divine rings, and the training required was rigorous.

One needed the stamina to wield the divine power and continue dancing for days on end, as well as an unwavering heart.

I evidently had neither. No one expected me to become a maiden. Not my mother, not my sisters, nor even I myself.

There was nothing for me to do here. I questioned my existence day after day.

Until one day, a man visited the island.

He saw the power within me and taught me there were things that even I could do.

His Blue Flame softly enveloped me, and I was released.

Thus did I become Signa the Little Finger.

The Blue Flame's color changed, and it continues to reside within me, even now.

Sir Sazantos...
I exist for you and you alone.

text:
Fame—the desire for recognition. Ever seeking and delighting in praise from others.

And yet the only fruit this bears is the hollow dream of the moon one day producing its own light.

Those who desperately tried to shore up their feeble self-confidence in this way lost sight of themselves.

If this dark desire was removed from the world, perhaps...

text:
Power—the desire to rule. Ever seeking to suppress others in order to protect oneself.

And yet the only fruit this bears is the insatiable impulse to conquer even order itself, like an endless ocean.

Those who relied on their own strength were defeated by those yet stronger than them.

If this dark desire was removed from the world, perhaps...

text:
Wealth—the desire to possess. A primordial impulse, as old as the earth itself.

And yet the only fruit this bears is a fleeting mirage, as ungraspable as sand.

Both the Haves and Have-Nots were consumed by this fixation.

If this dark desire was removed from the world, perhaps...

text:
Was I born from light or darkness?

The stronger the light, the greater the shadow that springs from it.

Any attempt to illuminate the darkness only brings forth new shadows.

I came into this world alongside the dark, with the hope that I would become a great light.

As one who held the gift of the Blue Flame, they said it was my destiny to become the Flameguard, and my life was spent solely in pursuit of that goal.

But then I discovered The Defenders of Ash, which revealed the truth of what happened to my mother.

Even so, I didn't loathe my existence. Rather, I simply felt a deep yearning for her.

And at the same time, I noticed an unfillable void in my chest.

Yet the circumstances of my birth didn't change what I had to do.

Like the Blue Flame, I was to purge all evil. Nothing more, nothing less.

One day, I asked my Blue Flame this:

Are you truly righteous, having been born from such vile evil?

Orsa created Finis out of loneliness. And their final child, Galdera, killed her with the dark flame of greed.

So then, both the Blue Flame and the dark flame find their origin in Orsa, do they not?

When Ceraphina gave me her power, I felt the void inside me disappear. The dark flame must have been inside me, as well.

Time and time again, I've witnessed brilliant hearts bestowing people with hope.

And yet... Ruined voices cannot reach that light. Those who defy the darkness are cursed to sink into it.

Light opposes dark, and dark opposes light. They fear each other, fighting eternally.

But I obtained a flame of both light and dark. Nothing is impossible for me anymore.

I will wipe out all desire and lead these souls on the correct path.

text:
Lavore's Journal

Right around when Wishvale was attacked by a fierce monster, my wife suddenly fell ill and passed away.

I think some gruff Knight Ardante came and dealt with the thing, but I can't remember clearly because I had my hands full taking care of my infant daughter.

By the time I finally got a handle on things, that knight, Fried, had married Yusia and settled down here.

And get this—they already had a kid who was only a few months younger than my Stia! I gotta say, that knight sure moves fast.

Stia grew up playing with wood and hammers and things, so she developed a talent for carpentry at an early age.

After my wife passed away, Stia was everything to me.

One day, she told me she wanted to travel the world instead of following in my footsteps. I said I was against it, but deep down, I was thrilled.

No matter where she goes, she's bound to do well for herself.

I dunno, maybe I have too much faith in her. But who wouldn't want to see their beloved daughter make her mark on the world?

But then...Wishvale was burned to the ground. So she decided to put all her energy into restoring it.

I was sad to see her give up on her dream, but proud nonetheless. My daughter's as noble as they come. I desperately wanted to ruffle her hair and give her a great big hug, even though I know she would've been too pleased with that. But now...I can't do anything.

You were always by her side, <chara_name>. So you probably know her even better than I do.

You're already a fine carpenter, Stia! I'd vouch for that in a heartbeat.

It's up to you to decide how you want to use those skills. May you live your life without regrets.

My greatest wish is for you to be happy.

text:
Yusia's Journal

Back when a savage monster from the Frostlands attacked the town and Miss Yaya's family passed away, a young man named Fried appeared, leading a group of Knights Ardante.

He was a strong man with a kind heart. When the monster attacked the town, this knight stood his ground against it, using his body to shield us from its attacks until he was covered in wounds.

After the monster was defeated, he stayed here in town while he recuperated. At the time, I was helping at the church, so it fell to me to take care of him.

<chara_name>... Ever since you came into our world, he thought of nothing else. And of course, the same was true of me.

When you learned to hold your head up, when you started crawling, when you stood for the first time... We celebrated every achievement with unfettered delight. The first time you said "Mama," Fried was insistent that you'd said "Papa" instead.

And when you showed interest in that toy sword, he resolved to teach you everything he knew. Your practice matches started out as a game for him, but before long he was putting his heart and soul into it every single morning.

With all the twists and turns life throws at you, you never know what's going to come in handy.

But I'm sure you'll use your father's lessons to follow the path you've chosen.

If I had one wish, it would be that you don't fall captive to the past, and instead find a path that's right for you.

And...if I were allowed one more wish...

<chara_name>...

I'd want to make <favorite_dish_name> for you one last time.

text:
Yaya's Journal

I was born the daughter of Wishvale's priest, naturally following in my father's footsteps to inherit his position.

In time, I too married, had a child, and was blessed with a grandchild. Then, one day...our little town was attacked by a violent monster. Only my granddaughter managed to survive.

Even so, I never considered myself unfortunate. The gods decide our fates. We must count ourselves blessed just to be born and cross paths with others.

I know that. And yet...

"O Flamebringer, why do you refuse to shepherd me on to the next life despite my long years?"

That was not the first nor the second time I had asked that.

But then...

"You won't leave me, right, Grandma?"

I recall my granddaughter's anxious face, the only family I have left in this world.

Before I could think, I found myself replying, "I promise to watch over you always."

Perhaps because she witnessed so much death from a young age, not least of which her own family's, my granddaughter, Laurana, grew to be a stout believer who was always kind and considerate of others.

There are not many among the clergy who closely associate themselves with death. And so, I kept a silent watch over her as the years passed.

However, looking back on it now, I think Laurana was lonely.

I only realized that after I fell deathly ill.

Laurana got me the medicine I needed—expensive medicine that could not be purchased anywhere nearby. Her face was carved with grief and guilt as she gave it to me.

Oh, Laurana... I'm so sorry. The words I spoke to you then became a binding curse to you. That's what made you look so downcast.

Oh, Laurana... All are fated to die. There are none who can escape it.

Oh, Laurana... Your actions brought forth calamity. I'm sure you will say it was all for my sake. But...that sort of love is nothing more than your excessive attachment to me. Choosing me over the law is where you went wrong.

What may seem good to you may be evil to someone else. Never forget that.

In the end, I share your crime. I failed to do the right thing because you were too dear to me. That is my mistake, which I have already paid for.

And yet, Laurana... I want you to look toward tomorrow and live.

For the sake of your future... For the sake of everyone's future.

text:
Aelfric's ring was damaged in the war and had lost some of its power.

So I brought it to the land of the Sacred Flame in order to restore it.

It took a good many years, but over time, Aelfric's wounds healed.

Eventually, humans came to live in that land and built a town.

That town was Wishvale.

Ringbearer chosen, <chara_name>...

One day, you will read this journal.

Go to the place where the azure flamestones slumber.

There, the ring will bestow upon you a new power.

text:
History of Wishvale

This land was born in approximately Year of the Divine 100. Church records state that it was the birthplace of the Order of the Sacred Flame.

After the battle against the abhorrent god Galdera, Saint Graecia and his disciples built a shrine in this land flowing with holy power, and named it Wishvale, praying that it would bring peace to the world.

But it seems that great light drew evil to it like moths to a flame. After a hundred years or more had passed, monsters began to multiply, and many townspeople's lives were lost. It was then that Graecia's descendants left Wishvale in search for a more habitable environment.

Only the most faithful remained. In time, they moved from cave to cave to hide themselves from the monsters. In one such cave, they found an azure flamestone.

When they used it to produce fire, an azure flame lit their surroundings. Perhaps the holy power, which had long lay dormant, had taken refuge in this stone. The people recognized it at once as the light of the Sacred Flame itself. This happy accident is still celebrated to this day, in the form of the Day of Reverence and the Enkindling.

Around this time, the faith had established institutes all around the continent, leaving Wishvale all but forgotten, relegated to a footnote in history.

The land didn't come into prominence again until around Year of the Divine 1100, when the divine rings were discovered across Orsterra. For the Flamebringer's ring was found in a cave close by there, strangely encased in crystal...

And that is where all records of Wishvale abruptly end. As if the Flamebringer's ring was somehow trying to conceal its existence...

text:
Aelfric the Flamebringer sealed the dark god and continues to watch over us and guide us to this day.

That's what the scriptures say.

Every time I read it, I quaked in fear at the thought of the dark god sleeping deep below the earth.

I wondered if he might one day awaken and plunge the world into darkness once more.

Perhaps that was why I was gifted with the flame: to combat that darkness.

I dearly wished to do nothing more than live a peaceful life with my betrothed, but the church would not allow it. They feared the darkness more than I did. And so, they shut me away, lest they lose access to the flame.

It seems the more one fears the darkness, the more one's heart is consumed by it. Underneath their snow white vestments, they were stained through and through.

Was this a divine trial to punish me for turning my eyes away from the darkness? An admonition to remind me that my flame must be used to eradicate the dark?

At the end of a long, gloomy tunnel filled with untold violence, I finally saw a ray of light: my children. I resolved to entrust to them the hope I had lost. But soon after this resolution, one disappeared. They had not inherited the Flame's gift.

A frail light cannot survive in a sea of dark. The first child received the gift in abundance, as if possessed of a thousand flames at once. While the church's eyes were drawn to him, I tried to help the remaining two escape, putting my life on the line to do so.

One was sent to a holy island that was kept secret. The other I entrusted to one Knight Ardante. I pray that they live in freedom and happiness.

But the one who inherited the gift—the blazing light who must fight the darkness in my stead...

To you, I can only apologize. I'm sorry that you've been forced to bear everything. Please, if you can, use that power to change things—to change this world that's drenched in greed.

My heart has already become a nesting ground for darkness. And I'm sure those words will become nothing but a curse to you—a curse that will darken your flame. So, with the last of my strength, I've decided to erase all my memories.

I doubt these words will ever reach any of the children burned into my memory, but even so, I leave this to them.

Remember...
You can become anyone.
You can go anywhere.

As long as there is a flicker of hope in your heart...

No matter where you go...
Or what pain and sadness you face...

text:
The Defenders of Ash - Preface

"Prayer alone will not save the people."

The first pontiff, Odypus, wanted to create a Sacred Flame born of human hands. That desire was passed down in the church for generations.

We, the cardinals, carried out an experiment in secret to see what would happen if those without the Flame's gift were given the Sacred Flame.

After Sonzon's passing, there were none who could inherit the Flame, and so the Flameguard came to an end.

We must find a way to revive the Sacred Blue Flame if we are to uphold the authority of the church.

text:
The Defenders of Ash - Lab Report 1

Year 257, 2nd day of Aeber
Coded text found in former Pontiff Odypus's residence. Shared only among us direct disciples.

Year 313, 15th day of Dohter
Deciphered portion of code. Replicating Lanthorn seems to be difficult. Remainder of code to be left to successor.

Year 492, 1st day of Bifelgan
Attempted to create "night" with demihumans. Accurst transformation of a number of them resulted in annihilation of half of all life in the Flatlands.

Year 568, 21st day of Alephan
Pontiff’s faction learns of our existence.

Year 623, 4th day of Aelfric
Secured half of cardinal seats. Succeeded in gaining control of the pontiff's faction.

Year 1112, 28th day of Alephan
Crossford line verified to be descendants of Graecia and confirmed to have Flame's gift. Appointed new Flameguard.

Year 1132, 2nd day of Winnehild
Built the Cathedral of Ash.

Year 1261, 23rd day of Sealticge
Captured Crossford's affiliate, discovered not to have Flame’s gift. "Night" came despite using Lanthorn. Subject discarded.

Year 1325, 13th day of Balogar
Experiment resulted in failure.

Year 1357, 18th day of Dreisang
Experiment resulted in failure.

Year 1372, 21st day of Brand
Experiment resulted in failure.

Year 1411, 17th day of Draefendi
Test suspended due to Sonzon's accurst transition.

text:
The Defenders of Ash - Lab Report: Postscript

With the might of magic, the Kingdom of Bernstein opened the Gate of Finis to a tragic end.

We must ignore our greed and strive for peace...

And safeguard our power, no matter what turns to ash.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 1 - Alephan

In the beginning, the people of Orsterra did not know what it meant to think.

And so Alephan the Scholarking created the notion of "knowledge."

Thus, the people were able to pass on their experiences to future generations.
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 1 - Alephan

In the beginning, the people of Orsterra did not know what it meant to think.

And so Alephan the Scholarking created the notion of "knowledge."

Thus, the people were able to pass on their experiences to future generations.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 2 - Dreisang

Though the people learned of knowledge from Alephan, they did not know how to use it.

And so Dreisang the Archmagus showed them the way.

This he dubbed "wisdom."
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 2 - Dreisang

Though the people learned of knowledge from Alephan, they did not know how to use it.

And so Dreisang the Archmagus showed them the way.

This he dubbed "wisdom."

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 3 - Dohter

The mortal beings of Orsterra lost their lives to fiends one after the next.

And so Dohter the Charitable, with her merciful heart, leveraged the wisdom the people held to bring them "healing."
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 3 - Dohter

The mortal beings of Orsterra lost their lives to fiends one after the next.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 4 - Draefendi

With the power of healing, the people grew in number, and food became scarce.

And so Draefendi the Huntress bestowed them with skills such as the hunt.

Thus, the people were able to ensnare prey and stave off hunger.
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 4 - Draefendi

With the power of healing, the people grew in number, and food became scarce.

And so Draefendi the Huntress bestowed them with skills such as the hunt.

Thus, the people were able to ensnare prey and stave off hunger.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 5 - Winnehild

People realized that the hunting skills they used against monsters could be used against their fellow man.

And so Winnehild the Warbringer, hoping to prevent tragedy, bestowed them with the "strength" to defend themselves in the form of eight sacred treasures.

These treasures were later sealed upon a certain isle.
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 5 - Winnehild

People realized that the hunting skills they used against monsters could be used against their fellow man.

And so Winnehild the Warbringer, hoping to prevent tragedy, bestowed them with the "strength" to defend themselves in the form of eight sacred treasures.

These treasures were later sealed upon a certain isle.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 6 - Brand

At first, the people feared each other's strength and did not act.

But those who gained greater power craved more and began to dominate the weak.

And so Brand the Thunderblade, righteous of heart, granted the weak "protection" to safeguard themselves from their oppressors.
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 6 - Brand

At first, the people feared each other's strength and did not act.

But those who gained greater power craved more and began to dominate the weak.

And so Brand the Thunderblade, righteous of heart, granted the weak "protection" to safeguard themselves from their oppressors.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 7 - Sealticge

Though the weak had protection, the strong continued to subjugate others.

Lured in by the evil in people's hearts, darkness was born.

And so Sealticge, Lady of Grace, used "dance" to lure greed to the ends of the earth. Thus, the world was temporarily spared from darkness.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 8 - Steorra

Those who cowered in fear of the darkness began wondering what their future would hold.

And so Steorra the Starseer showed them how to learn of their "fate."
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 8 - Steorra

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 9 - Balogar

After learning of their fate, some knew hope, and others despair.

And so Balogar the Runeblade gave those who despaired "weapons" to carve their own path forward.

Thus, people changed their destiny. The sacred sword Fisteralda is said to be one of the weapons bestowed by Balogar.
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 9 - Balogar

After learning of their fate, some knew hope, and others despair.

And so Balogar the Runeblade gave those who despaired "weapons" to carve their own path forward.

Thus, people changed their destiny. The sacred sword Fisteralda is said to be one of the weapons bestowed by Balogar.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 10 - Aeber

Unbeknownst to the people, the darkness crept back into the world and began eating away at their hearts.

They began wielding their weapons not to change their fate, but to harm others for their own gain.

And so Aeber, Prince of Thieves, "stole" weapons from the evildoers in an effort to maintain balance in the world.
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 10 - Aeber

Unbeknownst to the people, the darkness crept back into the world and began eating away at their hearts.

They began wielding their weapons not to change their fate, but to harm others for their own gain.

And so Aeber, Prince of Thieves, "stole" weapons from the evildoers in an effort to maintain balance in the world.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 11 - Bifelgan

But the darkness was wise and quickly mastered the art of stealing.

To put an end to the rampant fighting and theft, Bifelgan the Trader bestowed the people with "commerce."

Thus, those who did not pay for the items they took would be punished.
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 11 - Bifelgan

But the darkness was wise and quickly mastered the art of stealing.

To put an end to the rampant fighting and theft, Bifelgan the Trader bestowed the people with "commerce."

Thus, those who did not pay for the items they took would be punished.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 12 - Aelfric

All the wisdom that had been bestowed upon mankind had been warped by darkness, and the world descended into chaos.

Thus, the gods realized that what people needed were not weapons or skills, but to tread the right path.

And so Aelfric the Flamebringer lit flames to "guide" people's hearts, and thus the twelve gods once again drove darkness from the land.
text_joined:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Book 12 - Aelfric

All the wisdom that had been bestowed upon mankind had been warped by darkness, and the world descended into chaos.

Thus, the gods realized that what people needed were not weapons or skills, but to tread the right path.

And so Aelfric the Flamebringer lit flames to "guide" people's hearts, and thus the twelve gods once again drove darkness from the land.

text:
The Twelve Heavenly Scriptures: Apocrypha

Aelfric brought the flame to every land to "guide" the people, but flames have ever been symbols of "wisdom" as well as "weapons." Of "strength" that controls "fate."

The stronger the flame, the more gravely it burns any who touch it. The stronger the flame, the more it scorches everything around it.

When everything turns to ash, all that will be left is darkness. That is the very moment the banished darkness has been waiting for—the darkness known as Galdera, the Fallen.

text:
Auguste's Journal

Heinen was a young merchant,
very accomplished for his age.
He wanted to go into business with me.
To sell my plays to the masses.
His eyes were filled
with hope and wild ambition.
That look interested me far more
than any business arrangement.
Feigning interest in his plan,
I invited him to my villa...
I bound his hands and feet
and impaled him on a skewer.
I had hoped to see
his sparkling eyes grow tainted.
But Heinen merely cried out in pain
and begged for his life.
It was all too...expected.
It made me want to vomit.
Thoroughly bored,
I buried him in the garden.
Alas...
I need something more thrilling than this.

Alissa.
My thirteenth maid.
She was attentive and carried herself
with pride, despite her station.
But I found myself fed up with her greed.
A simple country wench from the Cliftlands...
In the first month after employing her,
she spoke to me of coin three times.
I suppose it's just the way she is.
I kept her working close by,
all the while, plotting her final moments.
When the time was right,
I told Alissa I would increase her pay.
Her cheeks flushed crimson.
She was overjoyed at the news.
I told her I needed to visualize a scene,
then bound her hands and feet.
I began placing coins into her mouth,
one at a time.
I told her it was part of the performance
and she obeyed quietly.
With the twentieth coin,
she began to choke.
I gathered that she wanted to cry out,
but her mouth was too full of gold.
With the twenty-ninth coin,
she choked to death on her newfound riches.
I was satisfied.
I then buried her in the garden.
Thirteen maids...
Is that why the garden reeks?
Regardless...
I now need a new maid.

text:
Lord Scarabe's Journal

In those days, I was nothing more than a mere jeweler. I traveled the continent, selling my precious stones and minerals... But the most profitable by far was Sufrataljah's gold dust.

That is what first led me to approach that man.

The Hüseynov family ruled over Sufrataljah back then.

They held a monopoly on gold dust and built their vast fortune on it.

Even so, Julian, the head of the family, was a good man.

He shared their wealth with the people, bringing peace to the Silver Spring.

And so, I curried favor with Lord Julian. I visited him often and procured much gold dust for myself.

I even deigned to coddle his impertinent son.

I had one singular objective: to obtain the Silver Spring.

Finally, my plan was put in motion.

I hired mercenaries and cut off the trade routes.

Next, I murdered every single merchant who had connections with the Hüseynovs.

Meanwhile, I ensured that no food supplies reached the town.

Having no way to produce food for themselves, Sufrataljah slowly began to starve.

Further still, I killed every traveler who wandered in seeking food.

Thus, within a mere month the Hüseynovs sunk down to the status of any normal family.

Their wealth dissolved into fleeting grains of sand.

In order to save his people, Julian gave everything to me, who had earned his trust.

After which, I swiftly executed him, at long last obtaining what I truly desired:

the Silver Spring.

text:
The Legend of Saint Odypus

Saint Odypus was once a sinner.

After receiving a divine revelation from Saint Graecia, he decided to walk the path of the divine.

In his dreams, Graecia ordered Odypus to touch the Sacred Flame. The sinner was paralyzed at the thought of being burned, but...

Then a miracle happened.

The Sacred Flame burned away the worst parts of him—his sin, desires, and grudges.

His body was left without a single burn.

The Sinner was reborn as a Saint.

However...

"A hero who flies too close to the heavens will have their wings clipped and turn to ash."

Who could have expected such an accursed night to await...

text:
The Three Flames

The Sacred Flame burns blue.

It incinerates the darkness and cleanses the impure.

The fire we use burns red.

It warms and illuminates the dark, but conceals a terrifying, destructive power.

The Accurst Flame is the opposite of the Sacred Flame.

The Accurst Flame burns black.

It exists only to destroy, not bless.

Water, ice, mist... These three are all the same.

Just as these three flames are forms of the First Flame.

If the hearts of people are corrupted by darkness, so too will the Sacred Flame turn Accurst.

"A hero who flies too close to the heavens will have their wings clipped and turn to ash."

We must never forget that.

text:
The Accursed Night

Following the death of Saint Graecia, his teachings spread across the realm, and churches were built in every corner of the land.

Two hundred years later... Saint Odypus became the first pontiff in an effort to unite the scattered churches.

Odypus attained sainthood through Graecia's divine revelation.

He was also gifted in the arts.

In this way, those unable to read were finally given access to Saint Graecia's teachings.

He went on to create the Lanthorn such that anyone might wield the Sacred Flame.

And thus did the Flame travel the continent, marking the very first Kindling.

The people sung Saint Odypus's praises, regarding him with great reverence.

Yet every time he looked upon his devoted followers, a vile urge overcame him:

Greed.

He began to plot ways in which he might prove his superiority over his teacher and work miracles that would demonstrate his transcendence above mere mortals.

Finally, eighteen years after the Kindling, when the strength of each church's Flame was waning, he took the Lanthorn and seized the First Flame.

Using its sacred power, he performed a forbidden ritual such that he might become as a god.

However...

"A hero who flies too close to the heavens will have their wings clipped and turn to ash."

Odypus's greedy ambitions saw him devoured by the dark flame.

For he was found lacking... Of the divine ring and the divine blood...

text:
Salomon's Journal

In the beginning...

...there was no heaven or earth. There was only chaos.

In time, Orsa, Mother of All, was born. And so did She create our world.

Lamenting Her solitude, She created a companion in Her image. She named Him Finis, and with Him, bore thirteen children.

Upon giving birth to Her thirteenth and final child, Orsa drew Her final breath.

Bereft of His only companion, Finis began to weep. In His sorrow, He created a gate.

The Gate of Finis.

I devoted my entire life to this gate.

I was a scholar of the Kingdom of Bernstein.

One day, while surveying Duskbarrow's environs I came upon some ruins.

Which in turn pointed to the existence of the Gate of Finis.

A gate said to connect this world with that of the next.

I was fascinated by this prospect.

And so did I spend my years studying all the ruins of Orsterra, compiling all that I learned into a single book.

I titled it, From the Far Reaches of Hell.

However... Upon completing it, a terrible dread washed over me.

For its contents could turn the very world upside down.

I imagined that many would seek the Gate of Finis for any number of reasons.

And that fear quickly turned to reality.

Bernstein's king, Jennish, had set his eyes on my book.

Thus...did the king of Bernstein wage war with the Kingdom of Granatt, in order to gain access to the Gate of Finis which was located there.

The war dragged on, claiming countless lives.

Until Prince Beowulf of Ferian allied himself with Granatt.

Thus turning the tables against Bernstein.

And so...

King Jennish, finding himself in dire straits, made a horrific decision.

I committed a terrible crime.

After the Gate of Finis opened, monsters poured forth.

They devoured everything and everyone. Both the Bernstein and Granatt armies... The land, the commoners... Everything.

And so the war ended with the destruction of both kingdoms.

Afterwards...I heard that a sage managed to close the gate.

And I...continued to live on.

Why did I survive? I knew the answer to that question immediately.

The other survivors singled me out as the cause of it all.

And so the time has come for me to pay for my crimes.

My name will go down in history as the butcher who destroyed two kingdoms.

I am soon for the gallows.

But I am content with my lot. I will receive my due punishment.

And yet... I do have one lingering regret.

My one lingering regret... That is...

The little girl I will leave behind. My granddaughter, Ceraphina...

"A hero who flies too close to the heavens will have their wings clipped and turn to ash."

I hope you can forgive this old fool.

I'm so sorry, Ceraphina.

text:
Lyblac's Journal

I was so close! Aaah... Lord Galdera...

My plans were thwarted by the Chosen One and the Flameguard.

I descended upon Orsterra two hundred years ago, when that foolish king opened the Gate of Finis.

All to revive Lord Galdera...

He was the most powerful of all of Orsa's children, and yet his feeble, cowardly siblings managed to seal him away.

The worst among them being that damnable Aelfric with his power of the Sacred Flame and that ring... When he saw that he could not overcome Lord Galdera alone, the coward amassed other powers in the rings.

Lord Galdera... I beg your forgiveness. I was thwarted by that revolting Sacred Flame once more.

Still...

It seems the dark flame dispersed through Ceraphina has taken root in the false-blooded mongrel.

Yes... I can feel it. I can feel your sigh of relief, my lord.
text_joined:
Lyblac's Journal

I was so close! Aaah... Lord Galdera...

My plans were thwarted by the Chosen One and the Flameguard.

I descended upon Orsterra two hundred years ago, when that foolish king opened the Gate of Finis.

All to revive Lord Galdera...

He was the most powerful of all of Orsa's children, and yet his feeble, cowardly siblings managed to seal him away.

The worst among them being that damnable Aelfric with his power of the Sacred Flame and that ring... When he saw that he could not overcome Lord Galdera alone, the coward amassed other powers in the rings.

Lord Galdera... I beg your forgiveness. I was thwarted by that revolting Sacred Flame once more.

Still...

It seems the dark flame dispersed through Ceraphina has taken root in the false-blooded mongrel.

Yes... I can feel it. I can feel your sigh of relief, my lord.

And now that you've purr-oven yourself worthy, you can come to our island!

I bet you'll have no purr-oblem finding our little isle!

text:
You'd best stay away from the
Cyphlo River. The invaders are
going to attack from there.
text_joined:
You'd best stay away from the
Cyphlo River. The invaders are
going to attack from there.

text:
I can't even work thanks to them.
Hope this business gets cleared up
soon.
text_joined:
I can't even work thanks to them.
Hope this business gets cleared up
soon.

text:
Were there any more wounded by
the river? Let me know if you see any.
text_joined:
Were there any more wounded by
the river? Let me know if you see any.

text: I can at least help carry them.
text_joined: I can at least help carry them.

text:
I came to take advantage of
the chaos, but I never expected
it to be this bad.
text_joined:
I came to take advantage of
the chaos, but I never expected
it to be this bad.

text:
Might be a good time to try
makin' up for all I've done.
text_joined:
Might be a good time to try
makin' up for all I've done.

text:
Those invaders don't stand
a chance against my axe.
text_joined:
Those invaders don't stand
a chance against my axe.

text:
I've got no worries in the world!
C'mon, let's have a drink!
text_joined:
I've got no worries in the world!
C'mon, let's have a drink!

text: Wha!? Where are these invaders!?
text_joined: Wha!? Where are these invaders!?

text:
What? They're gone already!?
Why did no one wake me!?
text_joined:
What? They're gone already!?
Why did no one wake me!?

text:
The life of a fisherman is hard, but
seeing my partner's smile makes it
well worth it.
text_joined:
The life of a fisherman is hard, but
seeing my partner's smile makes it
well worth it.

text:
It's enough to make a man forget
all his troubles.
text_joined:
It's enough to make a man forget
all his troubles.

text:
...I can't die just yet. Not until they
get what they're due.
text_joined:
...I can't die just yet. Not until they
get what they're due.

text:
Donescu has thrived under
the blessing of the Cyphlo River.
text_joined:
Donescu has thrived under
the blessing of the Cyphlo River.

text:
But all that is fair must fade.
Our prosperity cannot last forever.
text_joined:
But all that is fair must fade.
Our prosperity cannot last forever.

text:
I can still hear the cries of children
who lost their families.
text_joined:
I can still hear the cries of children
who lost their families.

text:
I finally understand what it means
to lose a war.
text_joined:
I finally understand what it means
to lose a war.

text:
A war? Yes, I know...
Why do you think I'm hiding!?
text_joined:
A war? Yes, I know...
Why do you think I'm hiding!?

text:
Don't assume that everyone in
Donescu enjoys the fight!
text_joined:
Don't assume that everyone in
Donescu enjoys the fight!

text:
Ugh, I tried to tell them, but
they were too confident in their
military strength.
text_joined:
Ugh, I tried to tell them, but
they were too confident in their
military strength.

text:
If we had just surrendered, we may
not have suffered any casualties.
text_joined:
If we had just surrendered, we may
not have suffered any casualties.

text:
The people from the west
are attacking?
text_joined:
The people from the west
are attacking?

text:
But the soldiers will drive them off,
won't they?
text_joined:
But the soldiers will drive them off,
won't they?

text:
I have used all my worldly wealth
in pursuit of this kingdom's
restoration.
text_joined:
I have used all my worldly wealth
in pursuit of this kingdom's
restoration.

text:
But it is still not enough.
If only we were stronger...
text_joined:
But it is still not enough.
If only we were stronger...

text:
I am keeping a lookout now that
we have received a declaration
of war from G'roha.
text_joined:
I am keeping a lookout now that
we have received a declaration
of war from G'roha.

text:
I will not allow a single enemy
to enter our kingdom!
text_joined:
I will not allow a single enemy
to enter our kingdom!

text:
No enemies have been sighted
in the vicinity.
text_joined:
No enemies have been sighted
in the vicinity.

text:
...The only thing I see is our
devastated city.
text_joined:
...The only thing I see is our
devastated city.

text:
This is Donescu, the impregnable
fortress town.
text_joined:
This is Donescu, the impregnable
fortress town.

text:
This is Donescu. We may have
lost the battle, but we are not
defeated yet.
text_joined:
This is Donescu. We may have
lost the battle, but we are not
defeated yet.

text:
Everyone fought well. That is
the only reason this city has been
left standing.
text_joined:
Everyone fought well. That is
the only reason this city has been
left standing.

text:
Heh heh, war at last! Now I get to
put my skills to the test!
text_joined:
Heh heh, war at last! Now I get to
put my skills to the test!

text:
I'll make a name for myself, just
like the Unbending Blade!
text_joined:
I'll make a name for myself, just
like the Unbending Blade!

text:
Is this the true nature of war? Is
this the true meaning of survival
of the fittest!?
text_joined:
Is this the true nature of war? Is
this the true meaning of survival
of the fittest!?

text:
This is far from over, you bastards!
I'll make you bleed!
text_joined:
This is far from over, you bastards!
I'll make you bleed!

text:
Might you come by, traveler?
We're offering a discount.
text_joined:
Might you come by, traveler?
We're offering a discount.

text: Listen, everyone! The war is over!
text_joined: Listen, everyone! The war is over!

text:
We have to help everyone we can,
friend and foe alike! Anyone who
survived!
text_joined:
We have to help everyone we can,
friend and foe alike! Anyone who
survived!

text:
Edoras finally ends its conquest
and now this...
text_joined:
Edoras finally ends its conquest
and now this...

text:
Though I guess a syndicate member
like me shouldn't go on about the
virtues of peace.
text_joined:
Though I guess a syndicate member
like me shouldn't go on about the
virtues of peace.

text: Those bastards ruined everything.
text_joined: Those bastards ruined everything.

text:
But we can't afford to stand around
in a daze. We have to do all we can!
text_joined:
But we can't afford to stand around
in a daze. We have to do all we can!

text:
King Dudek is known far and wide
for his bravery.
text_joined:
King Dudek is known far and wide
for his bravery.

text:
Even if we were to go to war,
he would surely bring us victory.
text_joined:
Even if we were to go to war,
he would surely bring us victory.

text:
A body as tough as rock, and
a heart as gentle as the river...
text_joined:
A body as tough as rock, and
a heart as gentle as the river...

text:
He is a true hero, risking his life
to guard the young queen!
text_joined:
He is a true hero, risking his life
to guard the young queen!

text:
Dudek! Our great king! May history
sing your praises forever!
text_joined:
Dudek! Our great king! May history
sing your praises forever!

text:
I was once a priest of Saintsbridge.
Or, I was about to be ordained
as such.
text_joined:
I was once a priest of Saintsbridge.
Or, I was about to be ordained
as such.

text:
Until I was sent to this tiny kingdom.
The church leaders are poor judges
of character indeed.
text_joined:
Until I was sent to this tiny kingdom.
The church leaders are poor judges
of character indeed.

text:
*sigh* May the Sacred Flame
ever shine its warmth upon you.
text_joined:
*sigh* May the Sacred Flame
ever shine its warmth upon you.

text:
Light shines stronger in the dark.
Just as adversity binds people
together.
text_joined:
Light shines stronger in the dark.
Just as adversity binds people
together.

text:
Now is the time for us to join
hands and march ever forward.
May the Sacred Flame guide us.
text_joined:
Now is the time for us to join
hands and march ever forward.
May the Sacred Flame guide us.

text:
I'm waiting for my friend.
We always play together.
text_joined:
I'm waiting for my friend.
We always play together.

text:
I'm waiting for my friend.
We always play together.
text_joined:
I'm waiting for my friend.
We always play together.

text:
The stars tell of a great unavoidable
calamity!
text_joined:
The stars tell of a great unavoidable
calamity!

text:
A calamity comes from the west,
riding upon a black beast! Beware!
Take heed!
text_joined:
A calamity comes from the west,
riding upon a black beast! Beware!
Take heed!

text:
The great calamity has come,
just as the stars foretold!
text_joined:
The great calamity has come,
just as the stars foretold!

text:
An even greater trial yet awaits.
Beware! Take heed!
text_joined:
An even greater trial yet awaits.
Beware! Take heed!

text: Ruff!
text_joined: Ruff!

text:
We were attacked by some mighty
strong troops the other day.
text_joined:
We were attacked by some mighty
strong troops the other day.

text:
I made sure to hide as soon as I saw
them because what good am I to
anyone if I'm dead?
text_joined:
I made sure to hide as soon as I saw
them because what good am I to
anyone if I'm dead?

text:
How about it? Need some fur?
They're nice and warm!
text_joined:
How about it? Need some fur?
They're nice and warm!

text:
Damn Tytos, going on and on
about "protecting the town"
and then just up and dying.
text_joined:
Damn Tytos, going on and on
about "protecting the town"
and then just up and dying.

text: So much for "nothing's impossible!"
text_joined: So much for "nothing's impossible!"

text:
I want to get away from this town.
I don't have a place here anymore.
text_joined:
I want to get away from this town.
I don't have a place here anymore.

text:
But I don't have the money to travel...
I wonder if anyone would take me
with them?
text_joined:
But I don't have the money to travel...
I wonder if anyone would take me
with them?

text:
Many of the children here are
victims of the scarlet snow tragedy.
text_joined:
Many of the children here are
victims of the scarlet snow tragedy.

text: Phina healed their spirits.
text_joined: Phina healed their spirits.

text:
I heard Phina was the one controlling
the Black Scarlets.
text_joined:
I heard Phina was the one controlling
the Black Scarlets.

text:
I can't believe it. The children loved
her so much, too...
text_joined:
I can't believe it. The children loved
her so much, too...

text:
I was able to reopen my shop
thanks to you!
text_joined:
I was able to reopen my shop
thanks to you!

text:
You should be prepared to take on
even the toughest of monsters.
text_joined:
You should be prepared to take on
even the toughest of monsters.

text:
I-I am a Knight Ardante! You can
trust me to protect the town!
text_joined:
I-I am a Knight Ardante! You can
trust me to protect the town!

text:
I won't let a single monster in!
Or...at least I'll try not to...
text_joined:
I won't let a single monster in!
Or...at least I'll try not to...

text:
Feel free to take what's in that box
there for the reconstruction efforts!
text_joined:
Feel free to take what's in that box
there for the reconstruction efforts!

text:
Houses can be rebuilt. Tools can
be remade.
text_joined:
Houses can be rebuilt. Tools can
be remade.

text:
People can make anything happen,
so let's do all that we can.
text_joined:
People can make anything happen,
so let's do all that we can.

text:
There seem to be more and more
monsters lately. Let me know if you
see any nearby, okay?
text_joined:
There seem to be more and more
monsters lately. Let me know if you
see any nearby, okay?

text:
I can only hunt small game, but
I want to do what I can to help.
text_joined:
I can only hunt small game, but
I want to do what I can to help.

text:
I wasn't sure what would befall our
town when the scarlet snow fell,
but things have returned to normal.
text_joined:
I wasn't sure what would befall our
town when the scarlet snow fell,
but things have returned to normal.

text:
The Sacred Flame lights the path,
but it falls to the individual to
walk it.
text_joined:
The Sacred Flame lights the path,
but it falls to the individual to
walk it.

text:
One must reach the place to which
they are guided on their own two
feet.
text_joined:
One must reach the place to which
they are guided on their own two
feet.

text:
This memorial is dedicated to
Tytos and the people of the town.
text_joined:
This memorial is dedicated to
Tytos and the people of the town.

text:
The Cathedral of Tytos is further
ahead, so please drop by if you can.
text_joined:
The Cathedral of Tytos is further
ahead, so please drop by if you can.

text:
I'm stricken with nightmares every
night. I can't sleep at all.
text_joined:
I'm stricken with nightmares every
night. I can't sleep at all.

text:
Maybe some alcohol will help.
...It has to...
text_joined:
Maybe some alcohol will help.
...It has to...

text: Oh, have you seen my granddaughter?
text_joined: Oh, have you seen my granddaughter?

text:
She's a wonderful child.
If only she hadn't been forced
to experience such tragedy...
text_joined:
She's a wonderful child.
If only she hadn't been forced
to experience such tragedy...

text:
As legend has it, the Sacred Flame
can free people of their doubts.
text_joined:
As legend has it, the Sacred Flame
can free people of their doubts.

text:
Perhaps I'm still lost because
I don't pray enough...
text_joined:
Perhaps I'm still lost because
I don't pray enough...

text: ...I saw you with Rinyuu.
text_joined: ...I saw you with Rinyuu.

text: I won't let you have her!
text_joined: I won't let you have her!

text: I haven't seen Rinyuu lately.
text_joined: I haven't seen Rinyuu lately.

text:
I wonder if she doesn't like me
anymore...
text_joined:
I wonder if she doesn't like me
anymore...

text:
It all...it all burned down... I lost
everything... My manor, my fortune...
text_joined:
It all...it all burned down... I lost
everything... My manor, my fortune...

text:
I can't believe in any god...but I am
beginning to believe in the kindness
of people.
text_joined:
I can't believe in any god...but I am
beginning to believe in the kindness
of people.

text:
I thought the reconstruction efforts
would make me a profit, but I didn't
expect the town to be so unsavory...
text_joined:
I thought the reconstruction efforts
would make me a profit, but I didn't
expect the town to be so unsavory...

text:
Ah well, the more incidents there are,
the more business there is to be done.
text_joined:
Ah well, the more incidents there are,
the more business there is to be done.

text:
The hero Tytos once protected
this land from the Westerlings!
text_joined:
The hero Tytos once protected
this land from the Westerlings!

text: Now it's my turn to protect his cathedral!
text_joined: Now it's my turn to protect his cathedral!

text:
That story about this cathedral being
haunted... Do you believe it?
text_joined:
That story about this cathedral being
haunted... Do you believe it?

text: ...I-it can't be true, can it!?
text_joined: ...I-it can't be true, can it!?

text:
My, what a magnificent cathedral!
The decor is absolutely exquisite.
text_joined:
My, what a magnificent cathedral!
The decor is absolutely exquisite.

text:
It is thanks to the tireless efforts
of Bishop Tarset that we were able
to restore this place.
text_joined:
It is thanks to the tireless efforts
of Bishop Tarset that we were able
to restore this place.

text:
It is by the grace of the gods
that I can aid him in his work.
text_joined:
It is by the grace of the gods
that I can aid him in his work.

text:
Bishop Tarset was party to wrongdoing,
and yet I never noticed...
text_joined:
Bishop Tarset was party to wrongdoing,
and yet I never noticed...

text:
But he has been punished for his
misdeeds. And for that, we must
give thanks to the Flamebringer.
text_joined:
But he has been punished for his
misdeeds. And for that, we must
give thanks to the Flamebringer.

text:
The journey across the sands is
an arduous one. You should rest
here awhile.
text_joined:
The journey across the sands is
an arduous one. You should rest
here awhile.

text:
The hot sands can damage your gear.
You should really consider getting
some new equipment.
text_joined:
The hot sands can damage your gear.
You should really consider getting
some new equipment.

text:
You must be exhausted from
your journey! You should treat
yourself to a nice, cool drink!
text_joined:
You must be exhausted from
your journey! You should treat
yourself to a nice, cool drink!

text:
Make sure to stock up on what
you need. The desert can be a
brutal place.
text_joined:
Make sure to stock up on what
you need. The desert can be a
brutal place.

text:
This is hell...!
We don't deserve this...!
text_joined:
This is hell...!
We don't deserve this...!

text:
One day, I'll show those nobles...
Just you wait!
text_joined:
One day, I'll show those nobles...
Just you wait!

text: Did you hear? That awful lord is gone!
text_joined: Did you hear? That awful lord is gone!

text:
Looks like I wasn't the only one
who wanted a revolution!
text_joined:
Looks like I wasn't the only one
who wanted a revolution!

text:
I won't be sticking around here much
longer! I'm gettin' out of this hellhole!
text_joined:
I won't be sticking around here much
longer! I'm gettin' out of this hellhole!

text: The desert is so vast...
text_joined: The desert is so vast...

text:
If only this were platinum and
not sand...
text_joined:
If only this were platinum and
not sand...

text:
Leave this place if you know what's
good for you.
text_joined:
Leave this place if you know what's
good for you.

text:
Normal folk don't last long here
in Sufrataljah...
text_joined:
Normal folk don't last long here
in Sufrataljah...

text:
Just because one lord's gone,
it doesn't mean all is good.
text_joined:
Just because one lord's gone,
it doesn't mean all is good.

text:
Someone else will come and take
their place before long.
text_joined:
Someone else will come and take
their place before long.

text:
Nothing's changed here.
You can't escape your fate...
text_joined:
Nothing's changed here.
You can't escape your fate...

text:
My parents are gone, but I've got
lots of good friends.
text_joined:
My parents are gone, but I've got
lots of good friends.

text:
We're all on our own, so we help
each other out.
text_joined:
We're all on our own, so we help
each other out.

text:
This is a wealthy town...but those
blessings don't extend beyond our
borders.
text_joined:
This is a wealthy town...but those
blessings don't extend beyond our
borders.

text:
If there were more wells in the desert,
we could open up more routes; then
trade would flourish!
text_joined:
If there were more wells in the desert,
we could open up more routes; then
trade would flourish!

text:
Keep working, you maggots!
You've got a job to do!
text_joined:
Keep working, you maggots!
You've got a job to do!

text: ...Huh? What do you want?
text_joined: ...Huh? What do you want?

text:
Now that the lord's dead, everyone's
looking to take his place.
text_joined:
Now that the lord's dead, everyone's
looking to take his place.

text:
But you know who'll be the next ruler?
Me! Gahaha!
text_joined:
But you know who'll be the next ruler?
Me! Gahaha!

text: Feels good to be free!
text_joined: Feels good to be free!

text:
You interested in mining for gold dust?
It's not bad, I tell ya!
text_joined:
You interested in mining for gold dust?
It's not bad, I tell ya!

text:
I'm just a humble maid, looking for
a lord to serve...
text_joined:
I'm just a humble maid, looking for
a lord to serve...

text:
I'm just a humble maid, looking for
a lord to serve...
text_joined:
I'm just a humble maid, looking for
a lord to serve...

text:
But I fear there aren't any lords looking
for a humble maid to serve them...
text_joined:
But I fear there aren't any lords looking
for a humble maid to serve them...

text:
The Flamebringer rains blessings down
upon all.
text_joined:
The Flamebringer rains blessings down
upon all.

text:
Just as He does not discriminate, we
too, should strive to live in equality
with our fellow man.
text_joined:
Just as He does not discriminate, we
too, should strive to live in equality
with our fellow man.

text:
If you've come to mine gold dust,
why not work for me?
text_joined:
If you've come to mine gold dust,
why not work for me?

text:
You can share in my success.
How 'bout it?
text_joined:
You can share in my success.
How 'bout it?

text:
What's the matter? Is the way I use
a knife really so unusual?
text_joined:
What's the matter? Is the way I use
a knife really so unusual?

text:
This is how they were used long ago.
I just haven't changed my ways since
then...
text_joined:
This is how they were used long ago.
I just haven't changed my ways since
then...

text:
Nothing will change, no matter
who our next lord will be...
text_joined:
Nothing will change, no matter
who our next lord will be...

text:
The spirit of the Sand Sentinels lives on,
always.
text_joined:
The spirit of the Sand Sentinels lives on,
always.

text:
The previous lord's son was
a clever boy.
text_joined:
The previous lord's son was
a clever boy.

text:
He could predict the rain and survey
the land, even draw maps... He was
quite the prodigy.
text_joined:
He could predict the rain and survey
the land, even draw maps... He was
quite the prodigy.

text:
Everyone here believed that this town
would see peace. If only a certain event
had not transpired...
text_joined:
Everyone here believed that this town
would see peace. If only a certain event
had not transpired...

text:
I saw that! You were lookin' at me,
weren't ya? Got somethin' to say!?
text_joined:
I saw that! You were lookin' at me,
weren't ya? Got somethin' to say!?

text:
Grr! If you want a fight, you're close
to gettin' one, pal!
text_joined:
Grr! If you want a fight, you're close
to gettin' one, pal!

text:
Ho there! Where did you come from?
I hail from Riven!
text_joined:
Ho there! Where did you come from?
I hail from Riven!

text:
There are so many folks like me here,
trying to get ahead in this world...
Maybe I ought to go home.
text_joined:
There are so many folks like me here,
trying to get ahead in this world...
Maybe I ought to go home.

text:
As Lord Scarabe's guard, I shan't let
anyone lay a finger on him.
text_joined:
As Lord Scarabe's guard, I shan't let
anyone lay a finger on him.

text:
Fulfilling this duty is a warrior's pride,
no matter who their liege may be...
text_joined:
Fulfilling this duty is a warrior's pride,
no matter who their liege may be...

text:
A guard who cannot protect their liege
is a failure...
text_joined:
A guard who cannot protect their liege
is a failure...

text:
Perhaps I ought to return to the desert
to reclaim my pride...
text_joined:
Perhaps I ought to return to the desert
to reclaim my pride...

text:
Do you need something? If it's a bow
you're after, you'll have to look
elsewhere.
text_joined:
Do you need something? If it's a bow
you're after, you'll have to look
elsewhere.

text:
I don't make bows for anyone
outside the Detof clan.
text_joined:
I don't make bows for anyone
outside the Detof clan.

text:
Wha's that? Ye think I'm drunk, d'ya?
*hiccup*
text_joined:
Wha's that? Ye think I'm drunk, d'ya?
*hiccup*

text:
Course I am! That's what 'appens when
ya drink! Wha's so wrong with that?
text_joined:
Course I am! That's what 'appens when
ya drink! Wha's so wrong with that?

text:
Excuse me, but have you seen
my husband by any chance?
text_joined:
Excuse me, but have you seen
my husband by any chance?

text:
He leaves home whenever I scold him...
Perhaps I need to be more patient with
him...
text_joined:
He leaves home whenever I scold him...
Perhaps I need to be more patient with
him...

text:
